text: Hi there, I'm showing you in this video a real-time version of the visualization model I'm working on this last month and this is a model that I'm utilizing quite a lot to work with birdsongs and I actually began developing it as a solution for birdsong visualization and analysis and this is the first 100% real-time version that I created. I'm using the sloppy webcam that I have here and the microphone it has as an audio source and in this version the model is not designed to represent anything specific like I don't know specific features in the spectrogram frequencies whatever it's kind of a general-purpose one but it can actually do some cool stuff and some quite interesting thing so basically what the model does is to interpret and translate the audio as point and to distribute the points into 3d space like to create a special images of the sound and these points end up creating this kind of intricate layered networks and every point comes with with a subset of information embedded on it so every point has a color and the color indicates the most active frequency band at the moment of emission so it's not a precise frequency measurement it tells you more or less where the most energy is in the signal at every moment so it's like a frequency zone or tells you where the sound is going. Another value is the amplitude value on top of every point there is an emission time expressed in seconds on the bottom there is a relative lifetime also expressed in seconds it's an animated value and in this last version there is a new data tool on the left which is the spectral centroid expressed in kilohertz and the spectral centroid is like the center of mass of the spectrogram it's like an average of everything that's going on in the spectrogram at a certain moment of time so basically every point now has also the mean frequency of everything that is going on in the spectrogram and higher values suggest a brighter signal while darker values indicates a lower values indicate a darker one and yeah so you can tell how my usual voice range my normal voice range stays at about 200 Hertz but I can try to change the pitch with my voice so with some falsetto like and you can tell how this reflects into the model with changes in colors and shape distribution the shape of the points also so basically my voice is constantly shaping and reshaping the model over time in in space and another feature that I devised with this version is the possibility to save snapshots of the structure and this is an extremely interesting thing so basically at any moment I am able to freeze the real-time flow and have a create a still 3d model of it with all the data embedded of it so I can try to have a snapshot now and see how it looks like so yeah basically this is a 3d model of my voice we can call it that you can tell how the different values are for every point the distribution the different colors etc I can also navigate it a bit with this amazing zoom slider that I build for the occasion and you can also tell how the normal range of my voice has also higher frequency particles embedded on it which basically are the SSD sounds that I do when I talk and it's interesting to see how they are somehow sampled and inserted into the 3d model organically so I can switch back to the real-time generation at any moment and basically this is all data these are numbers data sets which can be saved and elaborated in many different ways they can be put into libraries they can be analytically compared they can be putting relationship one with the other and they can also be represented in different ways into graph and such so for instance in this version I built a couple of secondary graphs which are already into the touch designer network so I can have another snapshot now and see how they look like so yeah this is another 3d model of my voice and I built a graph that puts in relationship amplitude and frequency over time so here we have the amplitude values on the y-axis and the time window in which the snapshot has been taken on the x-axis which is 10 seconds but it can be virtually any time and the colored points are the frequency information for every point so basically what you see here you see here this is the same thing it's just data that is represented differently so basically you have a 3d model here with additional XY and Z information for every point and you have the same data here reported into a linear b-dimensional graph and you see how it looks like and another graph that I built puts in relationship the spectral centric values with the amplitude values and it's cool to see how the brightness of the signal can more or less relate with its amplitude so these are two possibilities of lots of possibilities that can be can be done with this model which basically is not just a simple visualizer is more like it can be a real-time audio analysis tool that can be used for a number of different purposes and the users are quite they can be very much and yeah I'm going to implement many other functions in the in the future work that I'm doing on this I have already many ideas of what to do and yeah now I can try something crazier with my voice like I can try to save a snapshot of this crap and see how it looks like okay you can see the normal range of my voice you can see my faceto range quite clearly and my whistle too that is embedded as a structure like per se into the overall model and I can see how it looks like in the graphs you can tell how my falsetto amplitude goes basically off scale in the graph but you can also clearly detect all the episodes in that I did mean my voice in the time dimension which is pretty cool and also I can see how the spectral centroid and amplitude relates this model so yeah this is where my work is the direction where my work is going at the moment and another very cool thing that I'm trying to do with this model is to feed it any audio source I can find because it can process every audio so basically whatever can produce a sound can be fed into it so I can try maybe some drawing or an almonica or the ringtones of my cell phone so this is basically how I'm wasting my time right now yeah this is like the state of the art of my work and and this is what I'm doing and as a further development on this model I'm going to introduce MIDI so I'm going to implement MIDI on it and I'm very curious to see where it can go because another project I'm very much involved now is the visual MIDI guitar and I'm seriously interested what on seeing what can be achieved with these different technologies put together and yeah I think also that when I be done with all the development work I try to talk a bit more in depth about how these models are actually built because the workflow is not really easy there are many things many techniques going together at once and the touch designer networks are rather colossal so it would require quite an effort to actually explain everything and also recording quality now is not really great because I'm going completely in real time and even if I have a rather powerful GPU is barely holding everything together so I'm doing what I can anyway yeah I guess that's it for now and if you want more of that you can like and subscribe as they say since more is to come I'm mostly active on Instagram but every now and then I come back to YouTube to post some development of my work I try to be more regular here and post more stuff and maybe also some some more creative thing and we'll see where it goes thanks a lot for watching and see you soon bye
